lifecycle_current: Active (renamed IBM MQ 2014; v9.4 released June 2024)
notes: Launched 1993; renamed WebSphere MQ 2002; renamed IBM MQ 2014. Active as of 2026. One of the most durable enterprise middleware products.

lifecycle_current: Active (CICS TS for z/OS 6.3 released September 2025)
notes: Introduced 1968; still actively developed for z/OS. CICS for IBM i sunsetted 2024-2025. Core z/OS product remains vibrant.
Developed by Transarc; folded into IBM TXSeries. DCE and Encina components removed in TXSeries V6.1 (2006).

lifecycle_current: Active under HCLSoftware (HCL Notes/Domino; v14.x as of 2024)
notes: IBM sold to HCL for $1.8B in 2019. HCL has invested in Domino revival. Market position greatly diminished vs. Microsoft 365.
DB2 became IBM Db2 in 2017. Remains a major enterprise RDBMS for z/OS and distributed platforms.
One of the oldest production database systems. Still used for mission-critical mainframe workloads. Regularly updated.
Bundled CICS and Encina. Later became IBM TXSeries for Multiplatforms. Encina component removed in 2006.
MQSeries integrated with PowerBuilder. PowerBuilder survived as SAP product; niche market.

lifecycle_current: Discontinued (superseded by IBM Rational and Eclipse-based tools)
notes: IBM VisualAge product line discontinued early 2000s; replaced by IBM Rational tools and Eclipse.

lifecycle_current: Dominant (evolved into web application and microservices architecture)
notes: The 3-tier Internet/Intranet architecture described became the dominant enterprise computing model.
